title: Add 30-minute lunch deduction to working-hour calculation

requirements.summary: Update working-hour calculation logic to automatically deduct 30 minutes for lunch on regular working days (leaveType = #noLeave), ensuring half-day and full-day leave entries are not affected
acceptanceCriteria: For a No Leave day, swipe-in 07:00 and swipe-out 16:00 (no breakfast) yields exactly 8h 30m net hours.
For a No Leave day, swipe-in 07:00 and swipe-out 16:00 with breakfast yields exactly 9h 00m net hours.
Half-Day First Half entries do not have 30 minutes deducted for lunch.
Half-Day Second Half entries do not have 30 minutes deducted for lunch.
Full-Day Leave entries still return 0 daily hours.
Smart Swipe-Out Prediction and Dashboard/Analytics screens reflect the updated net hours (lunch deduction applied) correctly.
All existing unit-level logic for clamping, breakfast bonus, and leave reductions remains intact.

file_operations.description: Update the calculateDailyHours function to apply a 30-minute lunch deduction for regular working days (leaveType = #noLeave). Modify the net hours formula to: Net Hours = (swipeOut − swipeIn, after clamping) − 30 min lunch + 30 min breakfast bonus (if breakfastAtOffice is true). Ensure the lunch deduction is only applied when leaveType is #noLeave and never for half-day leave entries (#halfDayFirstHalf, #halfDaySecondHalf) or full-day leave entries (#fullDayLeave). Preserve all existing logic for time parsing, work window clamping (7:00–18:30), breakfast bonus (+30 min), and leave-type-specific reductions. Add inline comments explaining the lunch deduction logic and its conditional application based on leave type.

Review and verify that the smart swipe-out prediction correctly reflects the updated working-hour calculation with the 30-minute lunch deduction. The component uses calculateTotalHoursForRange and calculateDailyHours indirectly, so ensure the predicted swipe-out time accounts for the lunch break when calculating remaining hours to reach the weekly target. No logic changes should be needed if hoursCalculation.ts is updated correctly, but add a test scenario comment to document that lunch deduction is factored into predictions for #noLeave days.

Review and verify that the Dashboard page correctly displays weekly and monthly summary stats using the updated working-hour calculation with lunch deduction. The page uses calculateTotalHoursForRange which depends on calculateDailyHours, so ensure weekly progress and on-track status reflect the new net hours formula. No code changes should be required if hoursCalculation.ts is updated correctly, but add a verification comment confirming lunch deduction is included in displayed hours for regular working days.

Review and verify that the Analytics page bar charts and summary statistics correctly reflect the updated working-hour calculation with lunch deduction. The page uses calculateDailyHours and calculateTotalHoursForRange to generate chart data and compute average/highest/lowest hours, so ensure all displayed metrics include the 30-minute lunch deduction for #noLeave days. No code changes should be required if hoursCalculation.ts is updated correctly, but add a verification comment confirming lunch deduction is factored into all analytics metrics.

Review and verify that the Export page generates CSV/TSV/XLSX files with attendance records showing the updated daily hours including the 30-minute lunch deduction. The page uses calculateDailyHours to compute hours for each record, so ensure exported data reflects the new net hours formula for #noLeave days. No code changes should be required if hoursCalculation.ts is updated correctly, but add a verification comment confirming exported hours include lunch deduction for regular working days.
